meta.title: Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 Engine Replacement Cost UK

Land Rover Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ126 engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,100–£1,900. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ126 supercharged V6 is rated ★★★☆☆. It offers a rewarding driving experience but has well-documented issues: water pump and cooling system failures, timing chain tensioner wear (especially pre-2015), and supercharger coupler wear. Cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6?

acceptedAnswer.text: Water pump failure, coolant crossover pipe leaks, timing chain tensioner wear (pre-2015 units), and supercharger coupler wear are the most common issues. None typically lead to full engine replacement if caught early — preventative cooling system maintenance is the priority.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a £15,000–£25,000 Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6, a £5,500 recon engine is a manageable 22–37% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. Given the Range Rover Sport's strong retained values, replacement is usually viable. Scrap only if the vehicle has suffered catastrophic damage well beyond the engine.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the 3.0 SC V6, the engine code is AJ126 — it's on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 3.0 SC badge signifies the 3.0-litre supercharged V6. If you're unsure, tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the AJ126's water pump, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the water pump is a straightforward repair costing £600–£1,200. The AJ126's water pump and coolant crossover pipes are the most common service items on this engine; replacing them as a preventative measure at 60,000 miles is strongly recommended.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — replace the pump and crossover pipes at 60,000 miles
If caught early; don't drive with a rattling chain
Straightforward coupler replacement; don't let it damage the supercharger
Replace plastic pipes with upgraded metal or OE parts
If head gasket or block damage has occurred, replacement may be cheaper
Not a common AJ126 failure; if it occurs, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with documented oil-change intervals
* Water pump and crossover pipes replaced as a preventative measure
* Post-2015 build with the revised timing chain tensioners
* Evidence of regular motorway driving (the supercharged V6 benefits from regular use)
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 unit with no timing chain history or cold-start rattle
* Service history shows repeated coolant top-ups with no diagnosis
* No record of water pump replacement on higher-mileage examples (60k+)
* Engine warning light present with no diagnosis documentation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The AJ126 supercharged V6 offers a rewarding driving experience in the Range Rover Sport, but it's a more complex engine than the diesel alternatives. Cooling system maintenance and timing chain tensioner health (especially pre-2015) are the keys to longevity. Buy on service history and proof of preventative cooling system work. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12-month warranty is the sweet spot.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport 3.0 SC V6 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain tensioner wear — especially pre-2015)
* Service History — Including which engine era (AJ126 is the only 3.0 SC V6 — verify this before purchase)
* Coolant Level — Is the coolant low with no visible leak? Potential water pump or crossover pipe issue
* Supercharger Noise — Any rattle from the front of the engine at idle? Supercharger coupler wear indicator
* Oil Leaks — Check the valley between cylinder banks for oil pooling (common on higher-mileage units)
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 5,000 miles? If so, investigate
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved cooling, timing, or fuelling issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; the supercharged V6 should pass easily if well-maintained

items.label: * Turbocharger
items.value: Supercharged (Eaton twin-vortices) — naturally aspirated base with supercharger

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: Cooling system issues (water pump, crossover pipes) — approximately 45% of AJ126 enquiries [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Summer — cooling system issues worsen in hot weather [PATTERN DATA]
